parsed.simulated_user_message: If I go, they'll probably stare at my hearing aids and make jokes about my weight. I'd just feel even more out of place and like no one wants to talk to me.

problems.summary: User is anxious about their new hearing aids and feels self-conscious about their body, especially after a weight comment at lunch made them feel judged. They are considering attending a photoshoot but fear negative attention, specifically that people will stare at their hearing aids and make jokes about their weight, making them feel even more out of place. The user feels stuck in a loop, believing that whether they attend the photoshoot or stay home, they will still experience loneliness.

User feels like everyone is judging them, and as a result, they stay alone. This feeling of being judged is exacerbating their sense of loneliness and invisibility, and is also tied to their concerns about their body image and fear of negative attention in social situations.
